articles.title: Intense heatwave causes illness among goats ahead of Eid al-Adha in Uttar Pradesh - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
In Moradabad, Uttar Pradesh, healthy goats being prepared for Qurbani, the ritual sacrifice of livestock during Eid al-Adha, have fallen ill due to an intense heatwave affecting the region.
Local caretaker Mohammed Kadir reported that his goat has stopped eating because of the rising temperatures. “My goat has a fever due to the prevailing heat; she is not even eating anything because of the rising temperature. There is immense difficulty,” he said.
Another caretaker, Mohammed Rauf, stated that his goat has suffered from loose motions since the previous night due to the heat. He also mentioned that some suppliers increase the animals’ weight by filling their stomachs with water, creating additional challenges. “Since yesterday, the goat has had loose motions because it is so hot. Some suppliers fill the goats’ stomachs with water to increase their weight, so there are a lot of difficulties,” he said.
Veterinary Officer Vikas Kumar explained that many animals are experiencing heat-related ailments such as high fever, dehydration, and diarrhoea. Treatment includes intravenous drips, antibiotics, and analgesics as necessary. “Due to the intense heatwave, the animals are catching high fever, dehydration problems, and even diarrhoea. The number of goats is high among the cases,” Kumar stated.
Eid al-Adha, also known as the Festival of Sacrifice, honours Prophet Ibrahim’s obedience to God. The central ritual involves the sacrifice of healthy livestock including sheep, goats, or cows, with the meat distributed to family, friends, and those in need.
To protect the animals amid the heat, Kumar advised owners to provide goats with ample water and avoid exposing them to sunlight for extended periods. “The owners need to give them more water and avoid taking them out in the heat… Drips are being administered as a part of treatment, and antibiotics and analgesics are being given if needed…” he added.
The India Meteorological Department has warned that heatwave to severe heatwave conditions are expected to continue across several districts of Uttar Pradesh, especially in the eastern parts of the state.
